scope: Residential lease disclosure/clause rules for San Francisco, CA and Seattle, WA
global_notes: This is a build scaffold, NOT legal advice and NOT a substitute for attorney review. Every record should be confirmed by landlord-tenant counsel in the relevant jurisdiction before it drives a generated lease.

verbatim_text is reproduced from public-domain government sources (federal regulation, CA codes, SF Admin Code, WA RCW). Where verbatim_required is true, the engine should insert the text exactly and, where noted, in the required font size.

cite_verified=true means the citation and/or text was confirmed against a primary or authoritative source during research. cite_verified=false means the citation is a best-estimate and MUST be checked (especially Seattle SMC section numbers and some CA Civil/Gov Code sections).
SF inherits all 'federal' and 'california' rules plus 'san_francisco' rules. Seattle inherits all 'federal' and 'washington' rules plus 'seattle' rules.
When two jurisdictions conflict (e.g. notice periods), apply the rule more protective of the tenant. This is encoded per-record in notes where relevant.

year_built.description: Year of construction / first certificate of occupancy

in_flood_zone.description: Unit in special flood hazard area (landlord actual knowledge)

has_periodic_pest_service.description: Property has contracted periodic pest control

shared_utility_metering.description: Tenant meter also serves areas outside the unit

is_rent_ordinance_covered.description: SF Rent Ordinance coverage (derived; see sf-coverage-determination)

ab1482_exempt.description: Unit exempt from CA Tenant Protection Act (AB 1482)

known_ordnance_within_mile.description: Landlord knows of former military ordnance within 1 mile

known_mold_hazard.description: Known mold exceeding limits or posing health threat

death_within_3yr.description: Death on premises within prior 3 years (excl. HIV/AIDS)

has_nonrefundable_fee.description: Any nonrefundable fee charged at signing or during tenancy

certificate_of_occupancy_date.description: Certificate of occupancy date (SF rent control / AB 1482 logic)

lifecycle_events.lease_signing: Rules with trigger_logic.always or field triggers evaluated at initial lease generation
lifecycle_events.rent_increase: Separate served notices, not body of initial lease (wa-rent-increase-form, seattle-edra-notice)

rules.name: Lead-Based Paint Disclosure + Lead Warning Statement
rules.verbatim_text: Housing built before 1978 may contain lead-based paint. Lead from paint, paint chips, and dust can pose health hazards if not managed properly. Lead exposure is especially harmful to young children and pregnant women. Before renting pre-1978 housing, lessors must disclose the presence of known lead-based paint and/or lead-based paint hazards in the dwelling. Lessees must also receive a federally approved pamphlet on lead poisoning prevention.
rules.attachment: EPA pamphlet 'Protect Your Family From Lead in Your Home' (current Jan 2026 version) - public domain: https://www.epa.gov/lead/protect-your-family-lead-your-home-english
rules.notes: EPA revised the DISCLOSURE form layout in April 2024. Also requires: landlord disclosure of known LBP/hazards, delivery of any existing reports, and signed acknowledgment retained 3 years.

rules.name: Megan's Law / Sex Offender Database Notice
rules.trigger: Every residential lease and rental agreement
rules.verbatim_text: Notice: Pursuant to Section 290.46 of the Penal Code, information about specified registered sex offenders is made available to the public via an Internet Web site maintained by the Department of Justice at www.meganslaw.ca.gov. Depending on an offender's criminal history, this information will include either the address at which the offender resides or the community of residence and ZIP Code in which he or she resides.
rules.notes: Required language in every lease. Insert verbatim.

rules.name: Tenant Protection Act (AB 1482) Rent Cap & Just Cause Notice
rules.statute_cite: Cal. Civ. Code §1946.2(f); rent cap at §1947.12
rules.verbatim_text: California law limits the amount your rent can be increased. See Section 1947.12 of the Civil Code for more information. California law also provides that after all of the tenants have continuously and lawfully occupied the property for 12 months or more or at least one of the tenants has continuously and lawfully occupied the property for 24 months or more, a landlord must provide a statement of cause in any notice to terminate a tenancy. See Section 1946.2 of the Civil Code for more information.
rules.notes: MUST be in at least 12-point type. EXEMPT units require different statutory exemption statement under §1947.12(d)(5).

rules.name: Shared Utility / Gas-Electric Metering Disclosure
rules.trigger: Tenant's gas or electric meter also serves areas outside the unit
rules.notes: Landlord must disclose shared-metering before signing and provide written agreement on allocation. Property-specific.

rules.name: Former Military Ordnance Location Disclosure
rules.trigger: Landlord knows of former federal/state ordnance location within 1 mile
rules.notes: Disclosure of known location only; property-specific.

rules.name: Mold Disclosure + State Mold Booklet
rules.trigger: Known mold hazard; booklet distribution applies broadly to prospective tenants
rules.notes: Written disclosure of known mold before lease; distribute state mold consumer booklet to prospective tenants.

rules.name: Bed Bug Information Disclosure
rules.notes: Pull current prescribed informational content from statute. Verify section number.

rules.name: Periodic Pest Control Service Disclosure
rules.trigger: Property has periodic/contracted pest control service
rules.attachment: Copy of pest control company notice (pesticides used, frequency)
rules.notes: Provide pest control operator's notice. Verify section number.

rules.name: Flood Hazard Disclosure
rules.trigger: Unit in flood zone and landlord has actual knowledge
rules.notes: Required since July 1, 2018 where landlord has actual knowledge of flood-zone status.

rules.name: Smoking Policy Disclosure
rules.trigger: All leases — state where smoking is prohibited/allowed
rules.notes: Designate smoking-allowed/prohibited areas. SF multi-unit smoking ordinances may add requirements.

rules.name: Death on Premises Disclosure
rules.trigger: Death on property within prior 3 years
rules.notes: HIV/AIDS-related deaths excluded from mandatory disclosure.

rules.name: Foreign-Language Translation of Lease
rules.trigger: Lease negotiated primarily in Spanish, Chinese, Korean, Tagalog, or Vietnamese
rules.attachment: Full translated copy of the lease in the negotiation language
rules.notes: If negotiation occurs in a covered language, furnish translated lease copy.

rules.name: Rent Ordinance Coverage Determination
rules.trigger: Run on every SF unit to set is_rent_ordinance_covered
rules.notes: COO on/before June 13, 1979 generally rent-controlled. Decision tree — not a single boolean. Feeds sf-rent-ordinance-disclosure and AB 1482 logic.

rules.name: SF Rent Ordinance Disclosure
rules.trigger: Unit subject to SF Rent Ordinance
rules.verbatim_text: This unit is a rental unit subject to the San Francisco Rent Ordinance, which limits evictions without just cause, and which states that any waiver by a tenant of their rights under the Rent Ordinance is void as contrary to public policy.
rules.notes: MUST appear in at least 12-point font. Required on listings since April 1, 2020; carry in lease.

rules.name: Buyout Agreement - Tenant Rights Disclosure
rules.attachment: SF Rent Board pre-buyout disclosure form (sfrb.org/node/9)
rules.notes: Not a lease clause — buyout workflows only.

rules.name: Mold Information Disclosure
rules.attachment: WA Dept of Health mold information
rules.notes: Information on controlling mold growth and limiting health risks.

rules.name: Fire Safety & Smoke Detection Notice
rules.notes: Smoke detectors, sprinklers, alarms, evacuation routes.

rules.name: Move-In Condition Checklist
rules.attachment: Signed, dated move-in checklist; each party gets a copy
rules.notes: No deposit may be collected without written signed checklist. Gate deposit collection on this.

rules.name: Security Deposit Terms, Receipt & Return
rules.notes: DEADLINE CONFLICT: sources show 21 days (RCW 59.18.280) vs 30 days (recent amendment / Seattle SDCI). Confirm current statutory number with counsel.

rules.name: Nonrefundable Fees Must Be Designated in Writing
rules.notes: Fee is refundable unless lease specifically designates it nonrefundable in writing.

rules.name: Statewide Rent Increase Notice Form (HB 1217)
rules.verbatim_text: FULL FORM NOT REPRODUCED HERE - sync from RCW 59.18.720(3). Pull complete current text from RCW and WA Dept of Commerce form.
rules.attachment: WA Dept of Commerce standardized rent-increase form + EDRA for Seattle (seattle-edra-notice)
rules.notes: 2026 statewide cap = 9.683%. No increase in first 12 months. State minimum 90 days notice; Seattle requires 180 days.

rules.name: Flood Hazard Disclosure + Renter's Insurance Notice
rules.notes: FUTURE-EFFECTIVE. Build now, gate on lease_start_date.

rules.name: Seattle Renter's Handbook (Required Attachment)
rules.notes: Printed copy required at INITIAL signing; digital permitted on renewal.

rules.name: 180-Day Rent Increase Notice Addendum
rules.notes: Seattle requires 180 days' written notice before housing cost increase — stricter than state 90 days.

rules.name: Economic Displacement Relocation Assistance (EDRA) Notice
rules.trigger: Housing cost increase of 10%+ within any 12-month period
rules.attachment: City EDRA notice (Amharic, Chinese Traditional, Somali, Spanish, Vietnamese)
rules.notes: Attach when housing costs rise 10%+ in 12 months.

rules.name: Just Cause Eviction Ordinance Language
rules.notes: Tenancy may only end for enumerated just cause; termination notices must carry just-cause language.

rules.name: RRIO Registration Number
rules.trigger: All Seattle rental units must be registered
rules.notes: Capture RRIO number as required listing/property field.

rules.name: Late Fee Cap & No Notice Fees
rules.notes: Late fee for rent capped at $10/month. No fees for preparation/delivery of landlord notices.

rules.name: Deposit / Fee Caps & Installment Rights
rules.trigger: Landlord collects deposit, last month, move-in fees, or pet deposit
rules.notes: Combined deposit + nonrefundable move-in fees ≤ one month's rent. Pet deposit ≤ 25% rent, 3 installments. 6+ month leases: installment rights for deposit + last month.

rules.name: Fair Chance Housing (Criminal History Limits)
rules.notes: Screening-stage rule, not lease body. Verify current enforcement post-2023 federal ruling.
